commands.review_summary:
The PR updates the CLI metadata preference test to use a spawnable Windows fixture and changes CI so macOS runs format/typecheck instead of the full type-aware oxlint check.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current main uses a Unix shebang fixture while `readCliMetadata()` spawns the artifact path directly, which is not a Windows-spawnable fixture. I did not run local Windows tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A maintainer should decide whether the macOS `pnpm check` bypass belongs in this PR now that CI is green; there is no clear automated code repair to queue.

target.title: test: make metadata fixture executable on Windows
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The Windows CI test for embedded CLI metadata fell back to stale sidecar metadata because the fixture artifact was a Unix shebang script without a Windows executable extension.

## Why This Change Was Made

Made the test fixture platform-aware by using a `.cmd` wrapper on Windows and preserving the Unix script path elsewhere.

## User Impact

Restores Windows CI coverage for preferring embedded metadata over sidecars.

## Evidence

- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`
- `pnpm format`
- `pnpm test -- tests/cli-metadata.test.ts`
- `pnpm check`
target.author: vincentkoc
